name: sidebar-container
role: Outer wrapper. Declares the container-query inline-size context AND owns the responsive layout switch via container query. Above the threshold: side-by-side flex. Below: stacked flex column.

side.note: Which side the aside renders on. Uses CSS Logical Properties (inline-start / inline-end) so RTL languages flip naturally.

threshold.note: Container-width threshold below which the layout stacks. narrow=40em, standard=60em, wide=80em.

meta.containerQueryContract: sidebar-container MUST have container-type: inline-size declared (either via the pack's CSS or operator-applied inline). v3.G.11 container-query convention applies.
meta.browserSupportBaseline: CSS container queries are Baseline 2024 (Chrome 105+, Safari 16+, Firefox 110+). Older browsers degrade to the stacked layout (no side-by-side); the layout still functions, just not responsive at the container level.

ariaPatterns.noLandmark: sidebar-container itself carries no role. The sidebar-aside slot uses <aside> (which has implicit role='complementary'); the sidebar-main slot uses <main> (which has implicit role='main') or <article> depending on the page context.

meta.cssRecipe: container-type: inline-size; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-section); @container (inline-size >= threshold) { flex-direction: row; }

name: sidebar-aside
role: The sidebar. Element is <aside> (implicit role='complementary'). Width set by the container's data-width. Always rendered first or last in DOM based on data-side (CSS controls visual side via order).

ariaPatterns.complementaryImplicit: <aside> has implicit role='complementary' — no role override needed. Operators may add aria-label='Documentation' or aria-labelledby for context.

name: sidebar-main
role: The main content area. Takes the remaining inline size beside the sidebar (or full width when stacked). Element is <main> for top-level pages or <div> when prim-sidebar is composed inside a larger landmark.

meta.minInlineSizeZero: min-inline-size: 0 is required for flex children to shrink below their content's intrinsic minimum (otherwise long unbreakable strings push the layout horizontally).

ariaPatterns.mainLandmarkOnePerPage: When using <main>, there must be exactly one per page. If prim-sidebar is composed inside a page that already has a <main>, switch sidebar-main's element to <div> or <article>.
